concerns.text: Task T016b (binary label conversion) is marked [P] (parallel-safe) but logically depends on T016 (3-state label mapping) to exist first. The binary column is derived from the 3-state column; T016b cannot run before T016 completes. This violates the 'Producer before Consumer' rule.

Task T031b (covariate adjustment) is marked [P] but explicitly depends on T024 (Model artifact) as noted in its description. Parallel tasks cannot depend on the output of a sequential task in the same phase. T031b must be ordered after T024, not parallel to it.

Task T034 (covariate adjustment) is marked [P] but explicitly depends on T024 (Model artifact). Similar to T031b, this creates a hidden ordering violation where a 'parallel' task requires a sequential artifact that hasn't been produced yet.

Task T017 (orchestrator) is marked [US1] but not [P], yet it is placed in the 'Implementation' block alongside T013-T016 which are marked [P]. The orchestrator logically depends on the completion of T013-T016. If T017 is intended to be parallel, it is incorrect; if sequential, it should not be grouped with parallel tasks or marked [P] implicitly by context.

Tasks T031b and T034 both implement 'covariate adjustment' (FR-012) and both depend on T024. T031b is marked [P] and T034 is marked [P]. This creates redundancy and potential conflict in the parallel execution model. The plan implies two distinct steps (standard permutation vs covariate adjustment), but the task descriptions overlap significantly, risking duplicate execution or confusion on which artifact is the 'producer' for downstream tasks.
